title: 1563rd meeting (June 2026) (DH) - Rule 9.1 - Communication from the applicant (24/02/2026) in the case of Hajzer and Others v. Hungary (Application No. 45016/20) (Laszlo Magyar group) [anglais uniquement]
text: SECRETARIAT / SECRÉTARIAT SECRETARIAT OF THE COMMITTEE OF MINISTERS SECRÉTARIAT DU COMITÉ DES MINISTRES Contact: Ireneusz Kondak Tel: 03.90.21.59.86 Date: 20/03/2026 DH-DD(2026)379 Document distributed under the sole responsibility of its author, without prejudice to the legal or political position of the Committee of Ministers. Meeting: 1563rd meeting (June 2026) (DH) Communication from the applicant (24/02/2026) in the case of Hajzer and Others v. Hungary (Application No. 45016/20) (Laszlo Magyar group) (appendices in Hungarian are available at the Secretariat upon request). Information made available under Rule 9.1 of the Rules of the Committee of Ministers for the supervision of the execution of judgments and of the terms of friendly settlements. * * * * * * * * * * * Document distribué sous la seule responsabilité de son auteur, sans préjuger de la position juridique ou politique du Comité des Ministres. Réunion : 1563e réunion (juin 2026) (DH) Communication du requérant (24/02/2026) relative à l’affaire Hajzer et autres c. Hongrie (requête n° 45016/20) (groupe Laszlo Magyar) (des annexes en hongrois sont disponibles auprès du Secrétariat sur demande) [anglais uniquement] Informations mises à disposition en vertu de la Règle 9.1 des Règles du Comité des Ministres pour la surveillance de l’exécution des arrêts et des termes des règlements amiables. DH-DD(2026)379: Rule 9.1: Communication from the applicant in Hajzer and Others v. Hungary. Document distributed under the sole responsibility of its author, without prejudice to the legal or political position of the Committee of Ministers. DGI 24 FEV. 2026 Dear European Council, SERVICE DE L’EXECUTION DES ARRETS DE LA CEDH European Court of Human Rights Council of Europe 67065 Strasbourg Cedex France Judgment and Request for Enforcement Lajos Hajzer Hungary 21 november, 1977 ECHR-LHUN 1.1 Lajos Hajzer v. Hungary 45016/20 I request the European Council to urge the Ministry of Justice of Hungary to implement the ECHRjudgment 45016/20 Hajzer v. Hungary. This judgment uniformly ruled that life imprisonment in Hungary must be reviewed after a maximum of 25 years. In my case, the ECHR found a violation more than a year ago, but the review has not taken place. The Convention, promulgated by Act XXXI of 1993, recognizes the jurisdiction of the ECHR as binding on Hungary under Article 3. Article 46(1) of the Convention: The Contracting Paities undertake to abide by the final judgment of the ECHR. Since the Chief Prosecutor's Office of Hungary, based on Section 649( 1) and Section 651 (3) of the Code of Criminai Procedure, did not ex officio order the enforcement of the ECHR judgment, I am initiating it myself through the European Council. DH-DD(2026)379: Rule 9.1: Communication from the applicant in Hajzer and Others v. Hungary. Document distributed under the sole responsibility of its author, without prejudice to the legal or political position of the Committee of Ministers. documents: ECHRjudgment 45016/20 Supreme Court ofHungary BfI 401/2002/3 Szeged, 28:.1 2.2025. Lajos Hajzer
case_name: CASE OF HAJZER AND OTHERS v. HUNGARY
